name: 10P/Tempel 2
expectedMagnitudeRange: ~7.0 at brightest -- binoculars or a small telescope under dark skies, not naked eye
visibilityWindow: Late July through August 2026, morning sky
viewingNote: Periodic comet (5.4-year orbit), closest to Earth Aug 3, 2026. Not expected to reach naked-eye brightness. Source: EarthSky 'Best comets of 2026', fetched 2026-07-18.

name: 210P/Christensen
expectedMagnitudeRange: Forecast magnitude 8-13.6 -- telescopic; comet brightness forecasts are notoriously uncertain
visibilityWindow: October-November 2026, brightening as it nears perihelion; southern-hemisphere sky before perihelion, northern-hemisphere sky after
viewingNote: Periodic comet (5.6-year orbit), closest to Earth Nov 8, 2026 (0.42 AU). Not expected to reach naked-eye brightness. Source: Cambridge 'Comet Prospects for 2026' (J.D. Shanklin); TheSkyLive -- fetched 2026-07-18.
